button.name: variant
button.description: Visual style variant of the button

button.name: size
button.description: Size of the button

button.name: disabled
button.description: Whether the button is disabled

button.name: loading
button.description: Whether the button is in a loading state

button.name: children
button.description: Content displayed inside the button

button.name: onClick
button.description: Click handler

button.name: fullWidth
button.description: Whether the button spans the full width of its container

button.name: inputMode
button.description:
Hints at the type of data that might be entered by the user while editing the element or its contents
@see {@link https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/interaction.html#input-modalities:-the-inputmode-attribute}

button.name: is
button.description:
Specify that a standard HTML element should behave like a defined custom built-in element
@see {@link https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/custom-elements.html#attr-is}

button.name: exportparts
button.description: @see {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/exportparts}

button.name: part
button.description: @see {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/part}

button.name: aria-activedescendant
button.description: Identifies the currently active element when DOM focus is on a composite widget, textbox, group, or application.

button.name: aria-atomic
button.description: Indicates whether assistive technologies will present all, or only parts of, the changed region based on the change notifications defined by the aria-relevant attribute.

button.name: aria-autocomplete
button.description:
Indicates whether inputting text could trigger display of one or more predictions of the user's intended value for an input and specifies how predictions would be
presented if they are made.

button.name: aria-braillelabel
button.description:
Defines a string value that labels the current element, which is intended to be converted into Braille.
@see aria-label.

button.name: aria-brailleroledescription
button.description:
Defines a human-readable, author-localized abbreviated description for the role of an element, which is intended to be converted into Braille.
@see aria-roledescription.

button.name: aria-checked
aria-colcount
button.description:
Defines the total number of columns in a table, grid, or treegrid.
@see aria-colindex.

button.name: aria-colindex
aria-colindextext
button.description:
Defines a human readable text alternative of aria-colindex.
@see aria-rowindextext.

button.name: aria-colspan
aria-controls
button.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current element.
@see aria-owns.

button.name: aria-current
button.description: Indicates the element that represents the current item within a container or set of related elements.

button.name: aria-describedby
button.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.
@see aria-labelledby

button.name: aria-description
button.description:
Defines a string value that describes or annotates the current element.
@see related aria-describedby.

button.name: aria-details
button.description:
Identifies the element that provides a detailed, extended description for the object.
@see aria-describedby.

button.name: aria-disabled
aria-dropeffect
button.description:
Indicates what functions can be performed when a dragged object is released on the drop target.
@deprecated in ARIA 1.1

button.name: aria-errormessage
aria-expanded
button.description: Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded or collapsed.

button.name: aria-flowto
button.description:
Identifies the next element (or elements) in an alternate reading order of content which, at the user's discretion,
allows assistive technology to override the general default of reading in document source order.

button.name: aria-grabbed
button.description:
Indicates an element's "grabbed" state in a drag-and-drop operation.
@deprecated in ARIA 1.1

button.name: aria-haspopup
button.description: Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element.

button.name: aria-hidden
button.description:
Indicates whether the element is exposed to an accessibility API.
@see aria-disabled.

button.name: aria-invalid
button.description:
Indicates the entered value does not conform to the format expected by the application.
@see aria-errormessage.

button.name: aria-keyshortcuts
button.description: Indicates keyboard shortcuts that an author has implemented to activate or give focus to an element.

button.name: aria-label
button.description:
Defines a string value that labels the current element.
@see aria-labelledby.

button.name: aria-labelledby
button.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.
@see aria-describedby.

button.name: aria-level
button.description: Defines the hierarchical level of an element within a structure.

button.name: aria-live
button.description: Indicates that an element will be updated, and describes the types of updates the user agents, assistive technologies, and user can expect from the live region.

button.name: aria-modal
button.description: Indicates whether an element is modal when displayed.

button.name: aria-multiline
button.description: Indicates whether a text box accepts multiple lines of input or only a single line.

button.name: aria-multiselectable
button.description: Indicates that the user may select more than one item from the current selectable descendants.

button.name: aria-orientation
button.description: Indicates whether the element's orientation is horizontal, vertical, or unknown/ambiguous.

button.name: aria-owns
aria-placeholder
button.description:
Defines a short hint (a word or short phrase) intended to aid the user with data entry when the control has no value.
A hint could be a sample value or a brief description of the expected format.

button.name: aria-posinset
button.description:
Defines an element's number or position in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.
@see aria-setsize.

button.name: aria-pressed
aria-readonly
button.description:
Indicates that the element is not editable, but is otherwise operable.
@see aria-disabled.

button.name: aria-relevant
button.description:
Indicates what notifications the user agent will trigger when the accessibility tree within a live region is modified.
@see aria-atomic.

button.name: aria-required
button.description: Indicates that user input is required on the element before a form may be submitted.

button.name: aria-roledescription
button.description: Defines a human-readable, author-localized description for the role of an element.

button.name: aria-rowcount
button.description:
Defines the total number of rows in a table, grid, or treegrid.
@see aria-rowindex.

button.name: aria-rowindex
aria-rowindextext
button.description:
Defines a human readable text alternative of aria-rowindex.
@see aria-colindextext.

button.name: aria-rowspan
aria-selected
aria-setsize
button.description:
Defines the number of items in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.
@see aria-posinset.

button.name: aria-sort
button.description: Indicates if items in a table or grid are sorted in ascending or descending order.

button.name: aria-valuemax
button.description: Defines the maximum allowed value for a range widget.

button.name: aria-valuemin
button.description: Defines the minimum allowed value for a range widget.

button.name: aria-valuenow
button.description:
Defines the current value for a range widget.
@see aria-valuetext.

button.name: aria-valuetext
button.description: Defines the human readable text alternative of aria-valuenow for a range widget.

button.name: onKeyPress
button.description: @deprecated Use `onKeyUp` or `onKeyDown` instead

button.name: onKeyPressCapture
button.description: @deprecated Use `onKeyUpCapture` or `onKeyDownCapture` instead

button.name: ref

input.name: value
input.description: Current value of the input

input.name: onChange
input.description: Callback fired when the input value changes

input.name: placeholder
input.description: Placeholder text shown when the input is empty

input.name: label
input.description: Label text displayed above the input

input.name: error
input.description: Error message displayed below the input

input.name: disabled
input.description: Whether the input is disabled

input.name: size
input.description: Size of the input field

input.name: type
input.description: HTML input type

card.name: title
card.description: Title displayed at the top of the card

card.name: description
card.description: Short description displayed below the title

card.name: imageUrl
card.description: URL of an image displayed at the top of the card

card.name: variant
card.description: Visual style variant of the card

card.name: padding
card.description: Internal padding of the card

card.name: hoverable
card.description: Whether the card has a hover lift effect

card.name: children
card.description: Content rendered inside the card body
